message.abstract:
<jats:title>SUMMARY</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  Antibodies with heavy chains that derive from the VH1-2 gene constitute some of the most potent SARS-CoV-2-neutralizing antibodies yet identified. To provide insight into whether these genetic similarities inform common modes of recognition, we determined structures of the SARS-CoV-2 spike in complex with three VH1-2-derived antibodies: 2-15, 2-43, and H4. All three utilized VH1-2-encoded motifs to recognize the receptor-binding domain (RBD), with heavy chain N53I enhancing binding and light chain tyrosines recognizing F486
                  <jats:sub>RBD</jats:sub>
                  . Despite these similarities, class members bound both RBD-up and -down conformations of the spike, with a subset of antibodies utilizing elongated CDRH3s to recognize glycan
                  <jats:italic>N</jats:italic>
                  343 on a neighboring RBD – a quaternary interaction accommodated by an increase in RBD separation of up to 12 Å. The VH1-2-antibody class thus utilizes modular recognition encoded by modular genetic elements to effect potent neutralization, with VH-gene component specifying recognition of RBD and CDRH3 component specifying quaternary interactions.
                </jats:p>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Highlights</jats:title>
                  <jats:list list-type="bullet">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Determine structures of VH1-2-derived antibodies 2-43, 2-15, and H4 in complex with SARS-CoV-2 spike</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Define a multi-donor VH1-2-antibody class with modular components for RBD and quaternary recognition</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Reveal structural basis of RBD-up and RBD-down recognition within the class</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Show somatic hypermutations and avidity to be critical for potency</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Delineate changes in spike conformation induced by CDRH3-mediated quaternary recognition</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:sec>
title: Modular basis for potent SARS-CoV-2 neutralization by a prevalent VH1-2-derived antibody class
